description_long: This is a World War II-era military bomber aircraft with a distinctive camouflage paint scheme in green and brown, featuring roundel insignias on the wings and fuselage. The airplane has a long, cylindrical fuselage with a glazed nose section, likely for a bombardier or navigator, and a cockpit positioned just behind the nose. It is equipped with four engines mounted on a straight, mid-mounted wing, each engine featuring a prominent propeller. The tail section includes a twin vertical stabilizer (H-tail) and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The fuselage displays the code 'EMOZ' in yellow, and the aircraft has visible gun turrets at the nose and tail, indicating its military function. The overall design and markings suggest it is modeled after a British heavy bomber, such as the Avro Lancaster.
description: A camouflaged, four-engine British military bomber with mid-mounted straight wings, twin tail fins, and roundel insignias. Features include a glazed nose, multiple gun turrets, and the code 'EMOZ' on the fuselage.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the camouflage pattern, roundels on the wings, and the full wingspan with four engines and propellers.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the full width of the wings with visible roundels.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underwing details, engine nacelles, and the roundels on the lower wings.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the glazed nose, cockpit, four propeller engines, and the symmetrical wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible sweep or winglets, slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable, tricycle landing gear with two main units and a nose gear (not fully visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and multiple window panels.
